senses.definition: a game for children in which one player closes or covers their eyes while others hide and then try to find where they are

examples.x: We played hide-and-seek in the park all afternoon.
She always hides in the same spot when we play hide-and-seek.
We used to play hide-and-seek in the backyard when we were kids.
It’s so much fun to play hide-and-seek, especially when everyone gets really creative with their hiding spots.
After a few rounds of hide-and-seek, we took a break and grabbed some snacks.

senses.definition: a game for children, in which each player throws a stone, marker, etc. on a squared pattern that was drawn on the ground and hops on them to pick it up

examples.x: We used to play hopscotch all afternoon when we were kids.
The sidewalk was perfect for drawing a hopscotch grid with chalk.
I couldn't keep my balance when I tried to hop on one foot during hopscotch.
My little brother loves playing hopscotch with his friends at the park.

senses.definition: a classic outdoor children's game where one player tries to find and tag the other players who are hiding, while the hiders attempt to run and kick a can before being tagged

examples.x: We used to play kick the can every summer when we were kids.
I almost got caught during kick the can, but I managed to sneak past the seeker.
Kick the can is a great game for a large group of friends to play outdoors.
We had to be really quiet during kick the can to avoid being spotted by the seeker.

senses.definition: a children’s game played by two or more children, in which one runs after the others and tries to touch them, the touched player then has to run after the others instead

a children's game where players sit in a circle, and one player walks around the outside, tapping others on the head and saying "duck" until choosing someone as "goose," who must then chase the first player around the circle before being tagged

a lawn game in which players take turns throwing bean bags or corn-filled bags toward a raised platform with a hole in it, aiming to score points by getting the bags into the hole or landing them on the platform

examples.x: We played cornhole at the family picnic, and it was so much fun.
I can never seem to get the bag in the hole when playing cornhole.
They brought a cornhole set to the beach, and we played all afternoon.
After a few rounds of cornhole, we took a break and grabbed some drinks.

senses.definition: a team-based outdoor game where players from two teams try to capture the opposing team's flag, located in their territory, and bring it back to their own base without being tagged or captured by the opposing team
a physical activity or game in which a person jumps over a swinging rope, either individually or with others, for exercise, fun, or as part of a rhythmic routine
a traditional outdoor game where participants hop or jump inside a large sack or burlap bag from a starting line to a finish line, competing to see who can reach the end the fastest

examples.x: We had so much fun during the sack race at the family picnic yesterday.
The kids laughed the whole time as they tried to jump in the sack race.
I could barely keep my balance during the sack race, but I managed to finish in second place.
They played a friendly sack race competition to see who could jump the fastest.
During the summer camp, the sack race was one of the kids' favorite activities.

senses.definition: a classic outdoor game where players take turns throwing horseshoes at a stake planted in the ground, aiming to encircle the stake or land the horseshoe close to it to score points

examples.x: We played horseshoes in the backyard during the barbecue.
He managed to get a ringer on his first throw in the game of horseshoes.
I’m not very good at horseshoes, but I had fun trying.
The kids watched while the adults played horseshoes in the garden.
After dinner, we went outside to play horseshoes under the stars.

senses.definition: a traditional Korean game where players kick a small object to keep it in the air without letting it touch the ground

examples.x: We played jegichagi in the park yesterday and tried to keep the object in the air as long as we could.
My friends taught me how to play jegichagi, and I was surprised at how hard it is to keep the object from falling.
At the festival, there was a jegichagi competition, and I joined in with some other kids.
I used to play jegichagi when I was younger, and I still remember the fun of trying to do tricks with it.

senses.definition: a casual game where players use their feet and other body parts to keep a small, round sack filled with beans or sand off the ground for as long as possible, often played in a circle or among friends for fun and skill-building

examples.x: We spent the afternoon playing hacky sack at the park with our friends.
I used to play hacky sack every day when I was younger.
She joined us for a game of hacky sack after the picnic.
The group of friends gathered in the park to play hacky sack and enjoy the sunny weather.

senses.definition: a children's game that involves using an elastic band looped around the ankles or legs of two players while a third player jumps over and performs various patterns and movements within the elastic loop
a team-based outdoor game where two teams compete to retrieve a designated object placed in the center and return it to their side without getting tagged by the opposing team

examples.x: We played a few rounds of steal the bacon at the family picnic.
The kids were so excited when their number was called in steal the bacon.
In steal the bacon, we had to sprint as fast as we could to grab the object.
During recess, the kids were playing steal the bacon in the schoolyard.

senses.definition: a competitive team game where two teams pull on opposite ends of a rope, aiming to drag the other team across a designated centerline or marker to win

examples.x: We played tug of war at the family picnic, and it was so much fun.
The kids were excited to compete in tug of war during sports day.
They decided to organize a tug of war tournament at the summer camp.
After a long battle, our team finally managed to pull the other team across the line in tug of war.

senses.definition: a children's outdoor game where two teams line up facing each other, and players take turns running to break through the opposing team's linked arms
a game for children in which a player is considered as a leader and other players must do what the leader says only if they add “Simon says” at the beginning of their instructive sentences

examples.x: I got out in the first round of Simon Says because I moved when Simon didn't say 'Simon says.'
During the game of Simon Says, I had to jump up and down whenever Simon said so.
Simon Says is always fun because you have to pay close attention to hear if Simon really says to do something.
The children were laughing so hard while playing Simon Says, especially when someone made a mistake.
We played Simon Says at the party, and everyone tried hard not to get caught doing the wrong thing.

senses.definition: a Finnish outdoor game where players throw a wooden pin, called the "mölkky," to knock down numbered wooden pins, trying to reach a specific score without going over, and it combines elements of bowling and accuracy
a traditional Korean outdoor game and performance art where participants use a see-saw-like wooden board to jump and perform acrobatic tricks, typically during festive events and celebrations

examples.x: We played neolttwigi at the festival, and I got so high in the air!
After watching a few people play neolttwigi, I decided to give it a try, and it was so much fun.
We spent the afternoon at the park, playing neolttwigi with friends and laughing the whole time.

senses.definition: a small, weighted fabric bag filled with beans or similar material, often used in various throwing or tossing games
